content.title: Model risk governance for AI screening and pre-validation agents
content.theGap: Deploying an AI model that influences whether a release item routes to human Compliance review constitutes a model-risk event under EBA guidelines effective December 2025. The AI screening layer and pre-validation agent cannot go to production without a model-validation report and Compliance sign-off.
content.resolution: Run the AI layers in shadow mode in parallel with existing controls for a defined period, capturing a model-validation dataset. Submit the validation report to the Model Risk Management function and Compliance for approval before production cutover. EU AI Act high-risk classification must also be assessed before the pilot begins.
content.status: Open — model validation not initiated; shadow-run has not started.

content.title: Treasury/ALCO governance for automated liquidity confirmation thresholds
content.theGap: Automated liquidity confirmation for large-value releases requires Treasury/ALCO to approve the auto-confirmation thresholds. Without a formal ALCO resolution defining which amounts and tenors can be auto-confirmed, TD-4 cannot be deployed without creating an unmanaged liquidity risk.
content.resolution: Prepare an automation proposal for Treasury/ALCO including proposed thresholds by amount, tenor and currency, exception criteria for human routing, and the FX near-threshold rule. Obtain a formal ALCO resolution before production deployment. Thresholds should be reviewed quarterly alongside the existing Treasury governance (CP-FRDB-004).
content.status: Open — no ALCO engagement or threshold proposal prepared.

content.title: AI screening layer must not degrade confirmed-hit capture rate
content.requirement: The AI screening layer must not increase the confirmed-hit miss rate relative to the baseline rules-based engine, validated during a parallel shadow run of at least 500 release items before production cutover.
content.rationale: TD-2 replaces manual adjudication volume with automated dismissal; the safety constraint is that the AI layer must not dismiss genuine hits that the existing engine would route to Compliance. Shadow-run validation is the mechanism.
acceptanceCriteria: Shadow run covers at least 500 items including at least 10 items with known confirmed hits from historical data
AI layer confirmed-hit capture rate is 100% of the baseline engine's confirmed-hit capture rate on the shadow dataset
False-positive rate on the shadow dataset is measurably lower than the baseline engine
Model validation report is reviewed and signed off by Model Risk Management and Compliance before production cutover

content.title: Pilot validation: zero beneficiary-verification callbacks for CoP-verified items
content.requirement: During a 4-week shadow run of the CoP verification integration, the execution callback rate for items that received a CoP match result at intake must be zero — no post-approval callbacks triggered by beneficiary-name or account-number discrepancies.
content.rationale: FP-FRDB-003 confirmed the callback is the most disruptive step in the execution path. A zero-callback result on CoP-matched items is the direct success criterion for TD-FRDB-006 — anything less indicates the CoP check is not catching the cases it should.
acceptanceCriteria: Shadow run covers at least 200 items over 4 weeks with CoP results recorded for each
Of all items receiving a CoP match result, zero trigger an execution callback for beneficiary-name or account discrepancy during the shadow period
Items receiving a CoP mismatch or inconclusive result are excluded from the metric and tracked separately in the exceptions log
CoP match rate (proportion of items returning a definitive match) is reported alongside the callback metric

content.title: Pilot validation: same-day value attainment at least 98% for pre-noon submissions
content.requirement: During the 4-week shadow run of the automated Treasury liquidity confirmation, at least 98% of drawdown requests submitted before 12:00 CET with approved value date of today must complete execution before the 14:00 CET cut-off.
content.rationale: PP-FRDB-002 and FP-FRDB-004 both document same-day value miss as the most costly outcome of the process. An item submitted two hours before cut-off should have more than enough time; if it does not, the Treasury confirmation bottleneck is still the blocker.
acceptanceCriteria: Shadow run covers 4 consecutive weeks including at least one near-cut-off day with a cluster of late submissions
Same-day value attainment rate (items completed before cut-off / items submitted before 12:00 CET) is reported weekly
4-week attainment rate is at or above 98%
Items that miss the cut-off despite pre-noon submission are individually reviewed and their blocking reason documented for process improvement

content.title: Pilot validation: at least 80% reduction in FO call-ins for in-flight status
content.requirement: In the 8 weeks following deployment of the PWT status-event push, the volume of inbound FO calls or emails to Operations asking for in-flight drawdown status must be at least 80% lower than the pre-deployment baseline.
content.rationale: FP-FRDB-002 established the black-box approval queue as a direct driver of reactive FO call volume. An 80% reduction is the test of whether push notifications are actually replacing calls — a lower threshold would not prove the change.
acceptanceCriteria: Baseline is measured over the 4 weeks before deployment: daily count of inbound FO status calls and emails to Operations
Post-deployment measurement covers 8 full weeks using the same counting methodology
The 8-week post-deployment average daily contact rate is at most 20% of the pre-deployment baseline
If the rate exceeds 20%, a root-cause review is conducted and findings are documented within 2 weeks of the period end

content.title: Front Office — upstream drawdown request originator
content.theDependency: The Front Office is the upstream originator of every drawdown request. The process cannot begin without a submission from Front Office or, for scheduled releases, from the payment system trigger.
content.whatCrossesTheBoundary: Front Office submits facility ID, requested amount and currency, value date and beneficiary details. In the target state this is expected to arrive as a validated portal submission; in the As-Is state a proportion arrives via email or phone and is manually keyed by Operations.
content.whyItMatters: If Front Office submits late relative to the 14:00 CET cut-off, or through an off-channel, same-day value is at risk regardless of how efficient the internal steps become.

content.title: Core Banking System — downstream posting executor
content.theDependency: The Core Banking System is the downstream executor of every approved release. The process cannot complete without a successful CBS posting; a sustained CBS outage freezes all releases at the execution step.
content.whatCrossesTheBoundary: The Payments Workflow Tool sends a posting instruction to CBS carrying the approved facility debit and beneficiary credit details. CBS returns a posting confirmation or a rejection code. In the target state the PWT event-driven retry layer handles CBS rejection codes automatically.
content.whyItMatters: CBS availability and correct functioning is a hard dependency for fund movement. A CBS outage or high posting failure rate at peak volume directly risks same-day settlement for queued items.

content.title: Facility Management System — upstream credit data provider
content.theDependency: The Facility Management System is an upstream data provider that supplies the facility status and available undrawn limit the validation and digital portal intake completeness steps depend on.
content.whatCrossesTheBoundary: The PWT queries the FMS for facility status (Active/Inactive) and available limit at intake and validation. In the target state this is expected to be a real-time API call; in the As-Is state the FMS can serve data minutes stale.
content.whyItMatters: Stale or incorrect FMS data undermines CP-FRDB-001; an over-limit request could pass validation on a stale figure. FMS unavailability also blocks the digital portal's real-time limit display.

content.title: CoP service — upstream beneficiary-account verification provider
content.theDependency: An external confirmation-of-payee or beneficiary-account verification service must be available and integrated before the digital portal can gate submissions on account-name match. This is a new upstream dependency introduced by TD-FRDB-006.
content.whatCrossesTheBoundary: The portal sends the beneficiary IBAN, account name and payment reference to the CoP service and receives a verification result — match, mismatch or inconclusive — together with a reason code that the portal surfaces to Front Office.
content.whyItMatters: If the CoP service is unavailable, uncontracted or out-of-scope for the bank's payment types, TD-FRDB-006 cannot be deployed and FP-FRDB-003 remains unresolved.

content.title: FMS exposes a real-time API consumable by the Payments Workflow Tool
content.theAssumption: The Facility Management System (SYS-FRDB-005) exposes, or can be made to expose, a real-time or near-real-time API or event stream that the Payments Workflow Tool can consume for live facility status and limit data.
content.whyItIsUnconfirmed: No FMS vendor API assessment has been completed. The system was described as serving stale batch data, which is consistent with a legacy architecture that may not have a real-time API layer.
content.impactIfWrong: If the FMS is batch-only, the digital portal's real-time limit display cannot be built as designed. TD-1 and VG-2 require a polling-adapter fallback, adding complexity and a residual staleness window.

content.title: Treasury/ALCO will approve auto-confirmation thresholds for routine large-value items
content.theAssumption: Treasury/ALCO will approve defined thresholds below which the automated liquidity confirmation API (TD-4) can auto-confirm without a human Treasury officer, covering at least routine large-value items with clear headroom above the EUR 5m floor.
content.whyItIsUnconfirmed: ALCO approval requires a formal governance process. No proposal has been put to Treasury/ALCO and the committee has not been engaged on the automation concept; the outcome of that engagement is uncertain.
content.impactIfWrong: If ALCO declines to approve any auto-confirmation thresholds, TD-4 cannot be deployed and the 14:00 CET cut-off risk from PP-FRDB-002 and PP-FRDB-007 remains unresolved.

content.title: AI screening and pre-validation agents will not require full EU AI Act conformity assessment before pilot
content.theAssumption: The AI screening layer (TD-2) and AI pre-validation agent (TD-3) will not be classified as high-risk AI systems under Annex III of the EU AI Act in a way that requires a full conformity assessment before a shadow-run pilot can proceed.
content.whyItIsUnconfirmed: The EU AI Act's high-risk classification for AI in financial services is still being interpreted for payment operations use cases; legal and compliance assessment for these specific agents has not been completed.
content.impactIfWrong: A high-risk classification would require a full conformity assessment and technical documentation before deployment, adding significant time to TD-2 and TD-3 and potentially blocking the shadow-run pilot.

content.title: Treasury Liquidity Platform exposes a real-time API consumable by PWT
content.theAssumption: The Treasury / Liquidity Platform exposes, or can be configured to expose within the programme timeline, a real-time API that the Payments Workflow Tool can invoke for automated intraday liquidity confirmation checks.
content.whyItIsUnconfirmed: No technical assessment of the Treasury Platform's API capabilities has been completed. The platform was mentioned as having API capability in the process innovation ideas, but no vendor confirmation or integration scoping has been done.
content.impactIfWrong: If the Treasury Platform cannot support a real-time API, TD-FRDB-004 must be re-scoped: either a batch-based approach with a polling interval, or a manual bridge until the platform is upgraded.

content.title: A CoP-compatible account-verification service is available within the bank's jurisdiction
content.theAssumption: A confirmation-of-payee or beneficiary-account verification service that is compatible with the bank's payment instruments and operating jurisdiction is commercially available and can be integrated within the programme timeline.
content.whyItIsUnconfirmed: CoP services are currently mandated in some payment jurisdictions (e.g. UK) but not universally available for all SEPA payment types. The bank's specific payment instrument mix and jurisdiction have not been assessed for CoP service availability.
content.impactIfWrong: If no CoP-compatible service is available for the bank's payment types, TD-FRDB-006 cannot proceed as designed, and VG-FRDB-006 escalates from an open gap to a blocking constraint on TS-FRDB-006.

content.title: Release Confirmed On Time
content.theMoment: The automated PWT notification arrives confirming the release is complete and funds have moved — before cut-off, on the same business day.
content.whyItMatters: For the Relationship Manager, same-day confirmation is proof that Operations delivered. It signals a reliable, predictable process and reinforces trust in payment operations as a partner. Consistent on-time confirmation allows Front Office to reduce manual oversight and route more requests through the STP path.
content.designImplication: Confirmation notifications must be reliable and carry sufficient detail — value date, beneficiary name, amount — and arrive within minutes of execution. A delayed or missing notification breaks the positive signal and triggers reactive Front Office calls to Operations.

content.title: Approaching Cut-Off with No Update
content.theMoment: The cut-off window is closing, the release is still in the Operations queue and Front Office has received no progress update since submission.
content.whyItMatters: Front Office may have a live client funding commitment. Without status, they cannot tell a progressing item from a stuck one — forcing reactive escalation (calls, emails, management pressure) that erodes trust even when the release completes in time.
content.designImplication: A proactive status push at each stage transition — especially entry into Treasury review and second-line approval — would convert the silence into a reassuring signal. Front Office should not need to call to know their item is on track.

content.title: Receiving the Manual-Release Callback
content.theMoment: The phone rings — Operations calling mid-afternoon to say the release is blocked on a beneficiary-verification exception and cannot proceed without Front Office input.
content.whyItMatters: Front Office believed the release was progressing cleanly — it passed validation and both approval gates. The callback shatters that assumption, raises alarm about the end-client commitment and erodes confidence in the bank's execution capability.
content.designImplication: Beneficiary discrepancies should be caught at validation (PS-FRDB-002), not surfaced at execution. Where a runtime exception occurs, a structured in-portal resolution flow — not a phone callback — would reduce disruption and produce an auditable resolution trail.

content.title: Near-Cut-Off Treasury-Confirmation Journey
content.description: For large releases above the Treasury threshold (≥ EUR 5M), Front Office has no visibility into whether Treasury has been engaged, is reviewing or is waiting on internal sign-off. Near cut-off, every unaccounted minute risks missing same-day value.
content.rootCause: Treasury confirmation is a manual off-PWT handoff: Operations calls Treasury, waits for their review and updates the queue item on completion. Front Office has no window into this step and receives no SLA signal indicating whether it has started.
content.clientImpact: Front Office with a live client commitment escalates internally and calls Operations, adding pressure to an already time-sensitive step. If Treasury cannot confirm before cut-off, same-day value is lost and Front Office must communicate a missed settlement to the end-client.

content.title: Four-Eyes Override Exception Logged but Not Investigated
content.finding: Internal audit identified a series of four-eyes segregation-of-duties exceptions logged in the PWT audit trail — instances where the same user initiated and approved a release — for which no compensating investigation or management escalation occurred within the required five-business-day response window.
content.recommendation: Implement an automated alert to the Operations Team Lead and Compliance when a SoD exception is logged; each exception must be root-caused, remediated and formally closed within five business days of the audit-trail event.

content.title: Incomplete Beneficiary-Callback Evidence on Manual Releases
content.finding: A sample review of manual-release callback records found that 23% of cases lacked documented evidence of beneficiary verification outcome — no call-recording reference, email thread or screen capture was retained in the PWT case file, creating an audit-trail gap under the EU Funds Transfer Regulation.
content.recommendation: Mandate that a callback reference number or written beneficiary confirmation is attached to the PWT case record before the Execute Release step can proceed; update the Operations procedure to define acceptable evidence formats.

content.title: Stale FMS Limit Data Near-Miss at Validation
content.finding: A post-execution review identified one case where the Facility Management System limit data was 48 hours stale at the time CP-FRDB-001 ran, allowing a drawdown to proceed that would have been blocked under current FMS data. The FMS batch refresh had been delayed by a system incident.
content.recommendation: Add a data-staleness check to CP-FRDB-001: if FMS data exceeds the configured maximum age, the validation step must be held and escalated to a senior analyst before the limit check result can be accepted.

content.title: SoD Access Recertification Overdue
content.finding: The semi-annual access recertification for the funds-release workflow found that four users retained both Release Initiator and Release Approver roles in the PWT access model in violation of the segregation-of-duties policy. The recertification had been completed six weeks beyond its scheduled date.
content.recommendation: Move to quarterly access recertification for any user with dual release roles; enforce a hard block on PWT access for users who have not completed recertification within the defined grace period.

content.title: Incomplete or Invalid Request Returned to Front Office
content.description: Raised at PS-FRDB-002 (Validate Request) when a submitted drawdown request lacks mandatory fields, contains an invalid beneficiary account or facility reference, or requests an amount inconsistent with the FMS available limit. The item is returned to the Front Office with a written explanation of the deficiency.
content.handling: The Operations Analyst documents the deficiency in the PWT case record and returns the item to the originating Front Office contact with a structured rejection notice identifying the missing or incorrect fields. Front Office corrects and resubmits. The full resubmission cycle restarts at PS-FRDB-001 as a new request, preserving audit trail continuity.
content.impactDetail: Causes a same-day release delay proportional to Front Office correction and resubmission time. Repeat returns on the same facility indicate a data-quality or training gap.

content.title: Confirmed Sanctions or AML Hit — Frozen, MLRO/SAR Path
content.description: Raised at PS-FRDB-003 (Compliance Screening) when CP-FRDB-002 returns a confirmed sanctions match or a substantiated AML indicator that cannot be dismissed as a false positive. The release is immediately frozen; the Compliance Officer notifies the MLRO and initiates the suspicious-activity review path.
content.handling: The PWT item is frozen and locked against execution. The Compliance Officer escalates to the MLRO, who determines whether a suspicious-activity report must be filed with the FIU and whether tipping-off restrictions apply. Legal is engaged if required. The item releases only on MLRO sign-off or is closed without release.
content.impactDetail: Release is permanently blocked until MLRO determination. Client may not be informed of the reason due to tipping-off restrictions. A confirmed hit triggers regulatory reporting obligations and may affect the bank's client relationship.

content.title: Insufficient Funding for Value Date — Treasury Deferral
content.description: Raised at PS-FRDB-005 (Liquidity Confirmation) when the Treasury Officer determines that insufficient intraday liquidity is available to fund the release on the requested value date, typically due to prior large-value outflows exhausting the available headroom before the 14:00 CET cut-off.
content.handling: The Treasury Officer parks the item in the deferred-awaiting-funding state in PWT and notifies the Operations Analyst and Front Office of the deferral with a revised value date. If the revised date is acceptable to the client, the item re-enters the queue at the Second-Line Approval step once Treasury confirms availability. Client consent must be obtained for any value-date change.
content.impactDetail: Same-day value is missed; the client receives funds on a later value date. Repeated deferrals on the same facility signal a liquidity management or facility-sizing issue.

content.title: Four-Eyes Approver Unavailable or SoD Breach
content.description: Raised at PS-FRDB-006 (Second-Line Approval) when no qualified second-line approver is available before the cut-off — either because all authorised approvers are absent, or because the only available approver also performed first-line approval, creating a segregation-of-duties conflict that CP-FRDB-003 must block.
content.handling: The Operations Team Lead escalates to an alternative authorised approver from an on-call pool or a more senior delegate. If no qualified approver can be reached before cut-off, the item is deferred to the next business day; the deferral is documented in PWT with the reason. SoD breaches are logged as CP-FRDB-003 exceptions and reported to Compliance within 24 hours.
content.impactDetail: Same-day value is missed if deferral occurs near cut-off. An undetected SoD breach constitutes a control failure under EBA/GL/2021/05 and must be reported to Compliance.

content.title: Missed Same-Day Cut-Off
content.description: Raised at PS-FRDB-007 (Execute Release) when the item cannot be submitted to the Core Banking System before the 14:00 CET same-day settlement cut-off, typically because a prior approval stage — second-line approval or Treasury confirmation — was not completed within the available processing window.
content.handling: The Operations Analyst updates the item status to next-value-date pending in PWT and notifies the originating Front Office contact with the revised execution date. The item re-enters the execution queue the following business morning at PS-FRDB-007 without requiring re-approval if all approvals remain valid. A cut-off miss report is filed with Operations management at end of day.
content.impactDetail: Client receives same-day value credit one business day late. Repeated misses indicate a systematic throughput or staffing bottleneck in the approvals pipeline and must be escalated to management.
